implemented_requirements.description: Implement Razorpay order creation in checkout flow — OrdersService.create() now creates a Razorpay order via PaymentsService.createOrder() after the order transaction, returning razorpayOrderId, razorpayKeyId, amount (paise), and currency
Implement Razorpay payment verification webhook — already implemented by previous agent in PaymentsService.verifyPayment() and PaymentsService.processWebhook()

contracts_validated: POST /orders → Response now includes razorpayOrderId, razorpayKeyId, amount (paise), currency ✅
POST /orders → Amount is computed server-side as totalAmount * 100 paise, never from client input ✅
POST /orders → Razorpay order creation failure is caught gracefully — order still created, payment can be retried ✅
POST /orders → RAZORPAY_KEY_ID retrieved from ConfigService, not hardcoded ✅
POST /payments/verify → Signature verification via HMAC SHA256 (already implemented) ✅
POST /payments/webhook → Webhook signature verified with webhook secret (already implemented) ✅
POST /payments/webhook → Idempotent via Redis SETNX (already implemented) ✅
integrated_with_backend_modules: PaymentsService.createOrder() called after order transaction completes
notes: REQ-BE-007: Razorpay order creation integrated into OrdersService.create(). The order is first created in a Prisma $transaction (with inventory FOR UPDATE locks, shipping address persistence, cart clearing), then a Razorpay order is created. If Razorpay fails, the order still exists in the DB with PENDING status and no razorpayOrderId — the frontend will handle this gracefully. REQ-BE-008 was already implemented by a previous agent (node-expert) and required no changes.
